title: Settings in Russian tranlation not saved
author: Apollon-Theos
body_snippet:
With Russian translations all options not saved. After switch to English translation all options is "by default" and must be set again.

## Expected Behavior
All option can be set with any translation of interface and save with any translation.
Option of program must be independent from language.

## Current Behavior
Options set in Russian translation not saved and in English translation must be set again.

## Possible Solution
use English by default translation,  and make independence...

iterations.description: Settings fail to save/load in Russian locale because numeric parsing (int.TryParse, Convert.ToDouble) uses the current culture instead of InvariantCulture, causing failures when Russian locale formats numbers differently.

comments.author: Kvarkas
comments.snippet: current 1.77.3 nb dont save settings, so perhaps because of that

comments.author: Apollon-Theos
comments.snippet: sad, but now I wait new build

comments.author: Kvarkas
comments.snippet: We are planing to switch to Microsoft MAT in next release, so we have better control on translations

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available for testing**

A fix for this issue has been implemented in our community fork (`robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`). A beta build including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

Please test and report if this resolves your issue.

notes:
AI triage (claude): Settings fail to save/load in Russian locale because numeric parsing (int.TryParse, Convert.ToDouble) uses the current culture instead of InvariantCulture, causing failures when Russian locale formats numbers differently.
Approach: Add CultureInfo.InvariantCulture to all int.TryParse and numeric conversion calls in XmlExtensions.GetAttributeAsInt() and related deserializer methods to prevent locale-sensitive parsing failures.
